channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A Nigerian Scam Artist’s Rise Through Online Romance Fraud
item.description: Carlos Barragán’s excerpt from 'The Yahoo Boys' tells the story of Chibuike, a young Nigerian man who turned to online romance scams after struggling with low-paying work and minor hustles. Initially ...

item.title: Alexis Wilkins Files Defamation Lawsuit Against MS NOW Over Misleading FBI Story
item.description: Alexis Wilkins, the girlfriend of FBI Director Kash Patel, has filed a defamation lawsuit against the media outlet MS NOW. The lawsuit claims that MS NOW published false and reckless reports about Wil...

item.title: Federal Appeals Court Blocks Pentagon Ban on Transgender Servicemembers
item.description: A federal appeals court has blocked Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's ban on transgender individuals serving in the U.S. military, ruling that the policy is rooted in unconstitutional animus rather tha...

item.title: Government warns on rising smartphone use among children and expands digital safety education
item.description: The Tanzanian government has raised strong concern about the increasing use of smartphones among children, highlighting the risks linked to online abuse and exposure to harmful content. Speaking in Pa...

item.title: Joseph Kirati Recognized for Outstanding Photojournalism on Nyatwali Community Issues
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Joseph Kirati, Creative Lead at The Chanzo, has been honoured as Best Photojournalist of the Year 2025 at the Excellence in Journalism Awards Tanzania (EJAT) organized by the Media Cou...

item.title: Supreme Judicial Council dismisses PTI petition challenging CEC
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Supreme Judicial Council (SJC) on Monday formally dismissed the complaint filed by Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) against Chief Election Commissioner Sikandar Sultan Raja. The petition,...

item.title: Marilyn Monroe’s final photoshoot reveals her control over her public image and legacy
item.description: The article reflects on Marilyn Monroe’s final months in 1962 through the lens of a nude swimming pool photoshoot taken by photographer Lawrence Schiller during the filming of 'Something’s Got to Give...

item.title: Behind-the-scenes look at Morgan Freeman filming Driving Miss Daisy (1989)
item.description: This article revisits Morgan Freeman’s experience working on the set of the 1989 film Driving Miss Daisy, highlighting how the production marked a pivotal moment in his rise as a leading Hollywood act...

item.title: Research Explains How South Africa’s School System Helps Many Learners Reach Matric Despite Early Learning Gaps
item.description: A new study by researchers from Stellenbosch University, the University of Cape Town and the University of KwaZulu-Natal examines why many South African learners successfully reach Grade 12 even thoug...

item.title: Anthropic Confidentially Files for IPO as AI Company Race for Public Markets Intensifies
item.description: Anthropic, the artificial intelligence company behind the Claude chatbot, has confidentially submitted draft paperwork for an initial public offering, marking a significant milestone in the competitiv...

item.title: Scott Pelley Confronts CBS Leadership Over Mass Firings and Editorial Decisions
item.description: During a tense internal meeting at CBS News, veteran '60 Minutes' correspondent Scott Pelley openly criticized CBS News editor-in-chief Bari Weiss following the recent dismissal of several prominent s...

item.title: Concerns Over Escalation Pathways and Nuclear Risk in U.S. Policy Toward Iran
item.description: This analysis examines concerns about potential escalation in the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran, focusing on the risk that decision-making under political and military pressure c...

item.title: Qatar’s influence and PSG’s dominance in European football
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain’s recent retention of the Champions League trophy highlights not only sporting success but also the deep integration of Qatari state influence into European football. Four years af...

item.title: PM Shehbaz outlines industrial growth measures and economic plans for FY2026–27 budget
item.description: ISLAMABAD – Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Monday held a meeting with presidents of chambers of commerce and industries to discuss measures aimed at promoting industrial growth in Pakistan. The prem...

item.title: Roger Stone calls for firing Smithsonian staff over alleged historical error about Nixon
item.description: Roger Stone, a longtime ally of former President Donald Trump and self-described devotee of Richard Nixon, publicly demanded that Trump fire all employees of the Smithsonian Institution. Stone's state...

item.title: Pakistan, EU discuss strategic partnership and regional peace initiatives
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met EU’s top diplomat Kaja Kallas at PM House on Monday, highlighting Pakistan’s commitment to deepen cooperation with the European Union across multiple sectors. During ...

item.title: Armenia refuses Putin’s call for EU membership referendum amid rising tensions with Russia
item.description: Armenia don publicly reject the pressure from Russian President Vladimir Putin to hold a referendum on joining the European Union, as tensions between Yerevan and Moscow continue to rise. Armenian Pri...

item.title: Strong Winds Cause Abandonment of Te Aroha Horse Racing Event After Tardis Victory
item.description: On Monday, the horse racing meeting at Te Aroha was abandoned due to strong winds that caused several sections of the running rail to collapse. Despite the challenging weather, a notable performance c...

item.title: Residents and security clash at Laikipia Air Base over proposed US Ebola quarantine facility
item.description: Chaos erupted in Nanyuki after hundreds of residents attempted to march towards the Laikipia Air Base to protest against plans linked to a proposed United States Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya. Th...

item.title: Republican lawmaker faces backlash after suggesting Trump critics should leave the United States
item.description: A Republican lawmaker is facing widespread criticism after suggesting that Americans who disagree with President Donald Trump’s approach to his administration’s Iran-related military policy should lea...
